judgment.summary: UnitedHealth shows strong cash generation and positive analyst growth outlooks but is currently navigating low margins and sluggish year-over-year growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 7.23 beat the 6.60 estimate (9.5% surprise); next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Forward P/E 18.84 vs trailing 29.47 — suggests a more attractive valuation based on future earnings.
Free cash flow (TTM) $17.69B — demonstrates strong cash generation capabilities.
Total cash $31.23B — provides a substantial liquidity buffer.
Analyst growth est for current Q 18.9% — indicates high expected growth from analysts for the upcoming quarter.
negatives: Profit margin 2.7% — reflects very thin profitability.
Current ratio 0.80 and Quick ratio 0.72 — indicates potential difficulty meeting short-term obligations.
Revenue growth 2.0% and Earnings growth 0.7% — shows very slow year-over-year growth.
Quarterly earnings growth -0.2% — shows a slight recent decline in earnings.

news.paragraph: UnitedHealth is experiencing downward pressure following reports that Berkshire Hathaway has exited its stake in the company. The stock has also faced significant declines driven by rising medical costs and member mix issues that have pressured earnings. While the broader healthcare sector has seen some recent advances, UNH remains under scrutiny due to these fundamental headwinds.
